channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Device Action: Delete - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to delete devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, Linux, macOS, and Windows devices in Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: What is Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune manages users and devices, simplifies app management and automated policy deployment, and integrates with mobile threat defense. It connects to Managed Google Play, Apple tokens and certificates, and Teamviewer for remote assistance. Can use MDM or MAM to protect data, configure devices, and simplify access to company resources.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll iOS and iPadOS devices using user and device enrollment, automated device enrollment (DEP), and Apple Configurator in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Android device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android and Android Enterprise corporate-owned work profile, personally owned devices with a work profile, fully managed, AOSP, and dedicated devices in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Windows device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Windows devices using Automatic enrollment, Windows Autopilot, group policy, and co-management enrollment options in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: macOS device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll macOS devices using device enrollment, automated device enrollment (DEP), and Apple Configurator enrollment options in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: How to Configure the Intune Company Portal Apps, Company Portal Website, and Intune App - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how you can configure the Intune Company Portal apps, Company Portal website, and Intune app.

item.title: Use Remediations to Detect and Fix Support Issues - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about Remediations in Microsoft Intune, including what Remediations are and view any prerequisites and licensing requirements. Also, learn how to deploy built-in and custom remediation scripts, and learn how to monitor your scripts.

item.title: Device Action: Disable Activation Lock - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Microsoft Intune to disable Activation Lock on Apple devices.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Reports - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune provides specific report types with focused views that contain consistent and timely data.

item.title: Intune Graph API - Reports and Properties - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Intune reports and properties provided via Graph API.

item.title: Manage endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Security Administrators can use the Endpoint Security policies and profiles to focus on security configuration of devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Send Custom Notification - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune to create and send custom notifications to users of iOS/iPadOS and Android devices.

item.title: Linux device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Linux devices in Intune using the Intune app. Set an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Endpoint security in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage endpoint security in Microsoft Intune. Configure security policies, deploy baselines, and integrate with Defender for Endpoint to protect devices.

item.title: View report details for encryption status of devices managed with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Microsoft Intune admin center to view reports for device encryption status across macOS FileVault and Windows BitLocker encrypted devices that you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manage devices with endpoint security in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how Security Administrators can use the Endpoint Security node to view devices and manage them in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use Microsoft Tunnel VPN with Android devices that don't enroll with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add support for Mobile Application Management (MAM) for Android to the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway. Tunnel support for MAM expands access to your organizational resources for devices that can't or haven't enrolled with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Move from Android device administrator to mobile application management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to set up mobile application management, an alternative option to Android device administrator that focuses on app protection and doesn't require device enrollment.

item.title: Protect data and devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Intune capabilities that can help you protect your devices and data against unauthorized access and other threats.

item.title: Overview of Microsoft Surface Management Portal - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the features and capabilities of Microsoft Surface Management Portal.

item.title: Mobile Application Management (MAM) for unenrolled devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use mobile application management without enrollment to deploy apps, and protect organization data within the apps. Get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks for this enrollment option.

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out what's new in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: In development - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes Microsoft Intune features that are in development.

item.title: Step 3. Integrate Mobile Threat Defense for App Protection Policy - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 3. Integrate Mobile Threat Defense signals with Microsoft Edge for Business app protection policies in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Licenses available for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune is available with these licenses

item.title: Use Multi Admin Approval in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure Multi Admin Approval to protect your tenant against the use of compromised administrative accounts in Intune.

item.title: Get an Apple MDM Push certificate for Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an Apple MDM Push certificate to manage iOS/iPadOS devices with Intune.

item.title: Prepare a Win32 App to Be Uploaded to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to prepare a Win32 app to be uploaded to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Enable MDM automatic enrollment for Windows - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enable Intune automatic enrollment for Windows devices joining or registering with your Microsoft Entra ID.

item.title: Device Action: Wipe - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to wipe, or factory reset, devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for iOS/iPadOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Create a policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to configure thousands of settings for Windows 10/11, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, and Android client devices, including Microsoft Office apps, Microsoft Edge, administrative templates (ADMX), and more. Add these settings in a device configuration profile to secure devices, and control different programs and features. Use Microsoft Copilot to get What If analysis, and learn more about each setting.

item.title: Step 5 – Enroll devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, Linux, macOS, and Windows devices in Intune.

item.title: Add and Assign Win32 Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add, assign, and manage Win32 apps with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add corporate identifiers to Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add corporate identifiers (enrollment method, IMEI, and serial numbers) to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for macOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned Macs into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Supported Microsoft Intune Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article provides lists of support partner and Microsoft apps that are commonly used with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Unlicensed administrator access to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about unlicensed administrator access in Microsoft Intune, including default behavior for newer tenants and how to enable it for older tenants.

item.title: What Is App Management in Microsoft Intune? - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about client app management capabilities by platform for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add the Company Portal for macOS App - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add the macOS Company Portal app.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS direct enrollment - Apple Configurator-Setup Assistant - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Apple Configurator to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices with Setup Assistant.

item.title: Understand Microsoft Intune Management Extension - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand Microsoft Intune management extension for Windows.

item.title: Device Action: Retire - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to retire device using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up account driven Apple User Enrollment - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up account driven Apple User Enrollment for personal devices enrolling in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 1 for deploying or setting up Intune. The starting point is to review supported configurations, sign up for the trial, configure the custom domain name, add users and groups to Intune, assign licenses to users, manage roles, grant admin permissions, and set the MDM authority.

item.title: Get started with macOS endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune guide to set up and configure macOS devices from setup to creating policies and enrolling devices. You secure your corporate and organization-owned endpoints that are enrolled in Intune, and then deploy at scale with Apple Business Manager or Apple School Manager.

item.title: Tutorial - Use Apple Business to enroll iOS/iPadOS devices in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In this tutorial, you'll set up Apple corporate device enrollment features with Intune to enroll iOS/iPadOS devices purchased through Apple Business.

item.title: Add Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add apps to Microsoft Intune so you can assign apps to users and devices. Intune supports a wide range of app types.

item.title: Add and Assign Managed Google Play Apps to Android Enterprise Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand how to synchronize and assign apps to Android Enterprise devices from the Managed Google Play store.

item.title: Set up enrollment time grouping - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview and setup of the enrollment time grouping feature in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: App Configuration Policies for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use app configuration policies on an iOS/iPadOS or Android device in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Learn about Intune security baselines for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy security baselines that have preset and recommended configurations to the Windows devices you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Actions - Wipe, Lock, Locate, and More - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how to use Microsoft Intune to remotely manage, wipe, lock, restart, and secure Android, iOS/iPadOS, tvOS, visionOS, macOS, Windows, and ChromeOS devices. Learn about available device actions, prerequisites, and bulk actions for IT admins.

item.title: Shared iPad devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Shared iPad devices.

item.title: Set up local admin account creation and password management for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up macOS account configuration with LAPS through automatic device enrollment for macOS devices in Intune.

item.title: Discovered Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand details about the detected apps that Intune found on a device.

item.title: Set up enrollment for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up enrollment for macOS devices in Intune.

item.title: Add App Configuration Policies for Managed Android Enterprise Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use app configuration policies in Microsoft Intune to supply settings when users run a Managed Google Play app.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment for shared device mode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to set up Apple automated device enrollment for iOS/iPadOS devices in shared device mode, a feature of Microsoft Entra.

item.title: Use Microsoft Intune to manage software updates for supervised iOS/iPadOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to manage system updates for supervised iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Overview of Apple device enrollment in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Utilize Apple device enrollment to enroll and manage user-owned iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Authentication methods for Apple automated device enrollment - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Describes the Intune-supported authentication methods you can use with automated device enrollment.

item.title: Create device platform restrictions - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Restrict personally owned devices, and specific device platforms and OS versions from enrolling in Intune.

item.title: Set up user enrollment with Company Portal for iOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up the profile based Apple User Enrollment option for personal devices enrolling in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: A guide to managing iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Remote Lock - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the remote lock action in Microsoft Intune to lock a managed device that has a passcode or PIN.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage Android devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the recommended processes to manage Android devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage macOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the recommended processes to manage macOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Sync - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the device sync action in Intune to apply policy, app, and configuration updates to managed devices.

item.title: Turn on iOS/iPadOS supervised mode with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to turn on iOS/iPadOS supervised mode with Intune.

item.title: Android configuration list for Intune settings catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Microsoft Intune settings catalog to add, configure, or restrict features on Android devices. This article lists and describes the settings you can configure.

item.title: Use direct enrollment for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy and enroll macOS devices in Microsoft Intune using direct enrollment with Apple Configurator.

item.title: Admin checklist for iOS/iPadOS software updates in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidance and advice for administrators that create and manage software updated for iOS/iPadOS devices using Microsoft Intune. See sample policy for different industry scenarios, including shared devices, kiosk, manufacturing, and information worker.

item.title: Use EPM support approvals for file elevation requests with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage support approvals for elevation requests when you use Endpoint Privilege Management for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Monitor App Information and Assignments - Microsoft Intune
item.description: After you assign an app to users or devices, use this information to help you monitor the app's status.

item.title: Add an Android Line-of-Business App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add a Android line-of-business (LOB) app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Known Issues for Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure policies that define how Endpoint Privilege Management functions in your tenant, and behaviors when elevating files to run in administrative context.

item.title: Set up Apple device management in Intune for corporate-owned userless devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for corporate-owned userless iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Back up and restore iOS/iPadOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about backup and restore scenarios for iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Device Action: Restart - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to restart managed devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: App inventory for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use app inventory in Microsoft Intune to collect detailed application data from Windows devices with faster refresh cycles and richer metadata than Discovered apps.

item.title: List of settings for the Microsoft Edge security baseline in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View a list of the settings in the Microsoft Intune security baseline version 112 and later, for the Microsoft Edge browser. This list includes the default values for settings as found in the default configuration of the baseline.

item.title: Manage Apple mobile devices and tokens in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Sync devices, distribute devices, and re-enroll devices for Apple automated device enrollment on iOS/iPadOS in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up a macOS ADE token in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create, renew, and delete Apple enrollment program tokens for macOS automated device enrollment in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Overview of Apple Automated Device Enrollment for iOS/iPadOS in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about automated device enrollment (ADE) for Apple mobile devices in Microsoft Intune, including supported scenarios, key features, and how to get started.

item.title: Overview of Apple Automated Device Enrollment for macOS in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about automated device enrollment (ADE) for Mac computers in Microsoft Intune, including supported scenarios, key features, and how to get started.

item.title: Device Action: Remove Passcode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune remove passcode action helps you unlock devices remotely when users forget their passcodes. Discover how to use this feature.

item.title: Manage macOS ADE devices and tokens in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Sync devices, assign enrollment policies, and distribute Mac computers for Apple automated device enrollment in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use the Change Review Agent - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the Change Review Agent in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Apple School Manager - get Apple token and assign devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get the Apple token needed to set up Apple School Manager and Microsoft Intune for corporate-owned devices.

item.title: Set up an iOS/iPadOS ADE token in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create, renew, and delete Apple enrollment program tokens in Microsoft Intune for automated device enrollment on iOS/iPadOS.

item.title: Apple School Manager Program enrollment for iOS/iPadOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to set up Microsoft Intune with Apple School Manager for corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Apple School Manager - sync and distribute devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Sync and distribute Apple School Manager devices enrolled in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure the Intune service and set up the environment for education.

item.title: Get started with iOS/iPadOS frontline worker devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage frontline worker devices using iOS and iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune. Select the best enrollment option, configure the home screen, and more.

item.title: Set up just-in-time registration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up JIT registration in Intune for devices enrolling via a supported Apple device enrollment or user enrollment method.

item.title: Configure Apple account service access - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Apple access management settings to control how Apple accounts are used on organization‑owned devices managed by Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for tvOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned Apple TV devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).
